question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a block processing delay exceeding 500% of the 24-hour average block time by exploiting an exposed Spring Boot Actuator /env or /configprops endpoint to inflate the `frequency` property from its default 100ms to several seconds, causing the `@Scheduled(fixedDelayString = '#{@blockProperties.getFrequency().toMillis()}')` loop in CompositeBlockSource to poll for new blocks far less frequently than the network produces them, effectively stalling transaction visibility?
